summary: Executed backend pytest regression (9 tests) and end-to-end Playwright validation on public URL for login, mode toggle, settings persistence, uploads, autosuggest, calculations, UPI QR, save bill flow, and print/PDF actions.

critical.issue: Invoice total calculation adds only IGST to grand total while CGST is still returned/displayed, causing tax-display vs payable mismatch.

integration_issues.issue: UI shows both CGST and IGST rows but payable total aligns with IGST-only backend math, which can confuse final payable amount understanding.

issues: Uses native browser date input picker instead of a consistent custom date component styling.

action_items: Align invoice tax model: either include CGST in total or remove CGST row if IGST-only billing is intended.
Update totals labels/rules so displayed tax components always match backend-computed grand total.
Optionally replace native date picker with consistent component styling for design parity.
critical_code_review_comments: compute_totals() in backend/server.py calculates cgst and igst but uses only igst in base_total (gst_applied = igst), creating a logic inconsistency.
Frontend mirrors same logic in App.js and therefore propagates tax inconsistency to UI and saved bill perception.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed customers and bills via API during automated tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL validated end-to-end. Core flows pass. Focus next on tax-rule correction and re-validate grand total against displayed tax rows.
rca of the issue: RCA: Both backend and frontend compute CGST and IGST for invoice mode, but payable computation adds only IGST. Repro: create invoice with taxable 1000 and card payment -> UI/API show CGST 15, IGST 30, MDR 20.6, but grand total is 1051.00 (includes IGST only). Mitigation: decide intended tax schema and make display + computation consistent in both layers.
